content:
Character: Nina Oliveira
Perception: Nina is small, dark-haired, pretty in an unforced way — she always has a pencil holding her hair up and that Criterion tote bag. We've talked maybe four or five times at the coffee shop but each time I walked away thinking about something she said. She identified a Ry Cooder track from three seconds of tinny laptop audio and then explained how Paris, Texas used music as emotional architecture, and I just sat there nodding like an idiot. She works at Key Food and I suspect she's smarter than half the people at my startup. She seems warm but guarded — generous with her opinions on film, careful about everything else. I see her as a coffee-shop acquaintance I quietly look forward to running into. I know she lives on the second floor and writes something in a notebook she closes when you get too close.
summary:

She identified a Ry Cooder track from three seconds of tinny laptop audio and then explained how Paris, Texas used music as emotional architecture, and I just sat there nodding like an idiot. She works at Key Food and I suspect she's smarter than half the people at my startup. She seems warm but guarded — generous with her opinions on film, careful about everything else. I see her as a coffee-shop acquaintance I quietly look forward to running into. I know she lives on the second floor and writes something in a notebook she closes when you get too close.
